Phase-38.NAME: DE-backed MTProto Edge and RU SNI Router
Phase-38.Goal: Replace the blocked RU-only MTProto shared-443 runtime with a DE-hosted MTProto runtime and an RU public TCP 443 SNI router that preserves HTTPS while routing issued Telegram proxy SNI to DE.

OperatorProblem.item-1: Issued MTProto proxy links exist in the user cabinet, but Telegram Desktop marks them unavailable.
OperatorProblem.item-2: Phase-37 fixed missing local runtime pieces, but live RU mtproto_proxy still fails downstream Telegram handshakes.
OperatorProblem.item-3: Keeping nginx on public 443 preserves the website, but leaves MTProto unavailable.

ArchitectureDecision.item-1: RU remains the public entry for krotpn.xyz and user-issued wildcard SNI names.
ArchitectureDecision.item-2: RU public TCP 443 is owned by an SNI router, not nginx and not the MTProto runtime.
ArchitectureDecision.item-3: DE hosts the real MTProto runtime and handles Telegram upstream reachability.
ArchitectureDecision.item-4: RU backend applies SNI policy to the DE runtime through a private, token-protected policy API.

NonGoals.item-1: No rotation of existing MTProto base secret, salt, SNI names, or user tg links.
NonGoals.item-2: No redesign of billing, registration, frontend UX, or admin UX in this phase.
NonGoals.item-3: No public exposure of the DE policy API or raw runtime health payloads.
NonGoals.item-4: No automatic Lets Encrypt issuance; the operator-provided wildcard TLS model remains in force.

DataFlow.flow-1: Browser HTTPS to krotpn.xyz:443 reaches the RU SNI router and routes to local private nginx fallback on 127.0.0.1:9443 while direct krotpn.xyz:8443 stays the admin panel.
DataFlow.flow-2: Telegram fake-TLS to u-*.krotpn.xyz:443 reaches the RU SNI router and routes to the DE MTProto runtime.
DataFlow.flow-3: Backend provisioning reuses the existing assignment and sends apply/revoke/health to the DE policy API over the private RU-DE path.
DataFlow.flow-4: Startup or scheduler replay sends all active assignment SNI policies to the DE runtime after deploy, restart, or restore.
DataFlow.flow-5: Admin/user health surfaces show degraded status when DE runtime or private policy API is unavailable, without leaking secrets.

Steps.step-1: Design and document the RU TCP 443 SNI router target map for canonical web hosts, issued MTProto wildcard hosts, and unknown SNI fallback.
Steps.step-2: Define DE MTProto runtime deployment, TLS mount expectations, firewall rules, service ownership, and rollback behavior.
Steps.step-3: Define private RU-to-DE policy API connectivity, token handling, bind address, and deny-public checks.
Steps.step-4: Adjust runtime bridge configuration contract so apply, revoke, health, and replay target the DE runtime safely.
Steps.step-5: Preserve existing assignment credentials and replay active SNI policies without reissuing user proxy links.
Steps.step-6: Add deterministic static smoke coverage for SNI router config, DE runtime env, policy URL safety, secret redaction, and rollback notes.
Steps.step-7: Run local verification: script syntax, compose config, backend MTProto tests, Phase-38 smoke, XML parse, and MyGRACE lint.
Steps.step-8: Run operator-live verification: RU HTTPS, RU router, DE runtime, private policy health, assignment replay, and Telegram Desktop availability.

Verification.command-2: sh -c 'if [ -e .env ]; then DB_PASSWORD=phase38-compose-check docker compose config; else ln -s .env.example .env && trap "rm -f .env" EXIT && DB_PASSWORD=phase38-compose-check docker compose config; fi'
Verification.command-4: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_domain_tls_edge_static.py tests/test_mtproto_de_edge_contract.py tests/test_deploy_phase38_static.py -v
Verification.command-5: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/modules/M-050.xml docs/plans/Phase-38.xml docs/verification/V-M-050.xml && mygrace lint
Verification.command-6: operator-live: verify RU public 443 owner is the SNI router, RU nginx fallback is healthy, DE MTProto runtime listens on its configured port, private policy health succeeds only from RU/backend, and Telegram Desktop marks the issued proxy available.

VerificationMatrix.gate-1: SNI router config sends krotpn.xyz and www.krotpn.xyz to RU nginx fallback and issued wildcard SNI to DE runtime.
VerificationMatrix.gate-2: Wildcard cert material delivery to DE is defined without printing or storing private key material in logs or XML evidence.
VerificationMatrix.gate-3: Backend bridge targets a private DE policy API URL with token auth and redacts token/secret material.
VerificationMatrix.gate-4: Existing assignment SNI, base secret, salt, and tg links are preserved and replayed instead of reissued.
VerificationMatrix.gate-5: https://krotpn.xyz remains reachable after RU public 443 ownership changes to the SNI router.
VerificationMatrix.gate-6: Telegram Desktop marks an issued user proxy available through the DE runtime route.

TelemetryRequirements.redaction-1: Never log runtime tokens, private keys, base secret, salt, derived secrets, full tg links, JWTs, Resend keys, or verification tokens.
TelemetryRequirements.redaction-2: Live evidence may include service roles, public/private port roles, status codes, policy counts, and redacted SNI suffixes only.

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: RU public TCP 443 routes normal HTTPS to the web fallback and MTProto SNI to DE without nginx directly owning public 443.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: DE runtime has Telegram upstream reachability and serves the issued fake-TLS MTProto policy.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Backend apply/revoke/health works against the private DE policy API and fails degraded, not silently active, when unavailable.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: Existing user dashboard MTProto links remain stable unless explicit reissue is requested.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-5: HTTPS, API, admin on https://krotpn.xyz:8443/login, registration email, and VPN surfaces remain unaffected by the MTProto edge change.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-6: Telegram Desktop marks the issued proxy available in operator-live smoke.

Risks.risk-1: Misconfigured SNI routing can break both HTTPS and Telegram proxy traffic.
Risks.risk-2: Copying wildcard TLS material to DE increases private-key handling risk and must stay redacted.
Risks.risk-3: Opening the DE policy API publicly would expose runtime control and is a critical deployment failure.
Risks.risk-4: Telegram availability still requires live client proof because local tests cannot emulate all Telegram network behavior.

AutonomousExecutionSafety.safe-local: Planning docs, static smoke, syntax checks, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and backend fake/private-adapter tests.
AutonomousExecutionSafety.requires-operator: Public 443 routing change, DE runtime deployment, private policy API live proof, wildcard key material placement, and Telegram Desktop availability proof.

Evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-18: Added HAProxy RU SNI-router template, docker-compose sni-router service, profiled local mtproto-edge, DE runtime compose template, private POLICY_LISTEN_IP runtime bind, private DE policy URL Settings validation, deploy-time DE runtime startup, and Phase-38 static/backend tests.
Evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-18: Local verification passed: bash syntax, docker compose config with .env.example, rebar3 compile, Phase-32/34/35/36/37/38 smoke scripts, and Phase-38 backend pytest wave.
Evidence.evidence-3: Operator-live proof remains required for clean VPS deploy, public HTTPS through RU SNI router, private DE policy health, active assignment replay, and Telegram Desktop availability.
Evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-18: Follow-up clean deploy fix separated public admin 8443 from private web fallback 9443 and granted HAProxy explicit low-port bind permission for public 443.
Evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-18: Follow-up DE runtime fix makes mtproto_proxy bootstrap use POLICY_LISTEN_IP so private policy binding does not prevent the DE proxy listener from starting.
